new_classes.label_en: Scotch whisky with added soda water in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 30 61)
new_classes.label_de: Scotch Whisky mit Sodawasser in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 30 61)
new_classes.definition_en: A Scotch whisky (distilled and matured in Scotland, marketed at ≥ 40 % vol) to which soda water has been added, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres.
Carbonated water (water to which carbon dioxide has been added) used as an additive in mixed spirit preparations.
A quality borne by a spirit indicating that it has undergone an ageing/maturation process in accordance with the applicable product specification (e.g. Scotch whisky regulations).

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the four main distinguishing criteria found in the legal text: (1) Scotch whisky origin (distilled and matured in Scotland) via ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality; (2) maturation requirement via MaturationQuality; (3) presence of added soda water via hasPartSomeValuesFrom SodaWater; (4) small container (≤ 2 L) via hasPartSomeValuesFrom SmallContainer. The minimum alcoholic strength of 40 % vol is also captured via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. A minor gap (coverage ~0.85) exists because the legal text references a general Whisk(e)y definition (grain mash distillate) that ideally would be expressed through a grain-mash distillation process restriction shared across all 2208 30 xx codes; however, that parent-level constraint is better placed on a shared parent Whisky class rather than repeated here, and the existing SpiritousBeverage2208 parent only partially captures this. Additionally, the exact cereal-mash feedstock restriction and the distinction between bottle vs. other containers are not fully formalised in the current TBox.
